claims.claim_text: コネ社のエレベーター・エスカレーターメンテナンスビジネスは、一度設置した機器に対する長期保守契約（メンテナンス契約は受注残に含まれない）を通じて高いスイッチングコストを生み出し、競合他社が同等の粘着性ある顧客基盤を構築することが困難な構造にある
claims.evidence_from_transcript: Q4 2013 CEO Matti Alahuhta: 'We don't include maintenance contracts in our order books, so this means that this gives us a, let's say, good basis to start the new year.' / Q4 2014 CEO Henrik Ehrnrooth: 'Our ambition is naturally to stay ahead of that curve [service business growth].' / Maintenance is a separate market with 'different players also active there, a lot of small independents'—implying OEM lock-in advantage.
claims.speaker: CEO (Matti Alahuhta / Henrik Ehrnrooth)

claims.claim_text: コネ社は中国において幅広いセグメント（新設・改修・保守）と地理的フットプリント（全国主要都市）を持ち、特定セグメント・地域依存なく成長機会を捕捉できる構造的ポジションを構築している
claims.evidence_from_transcript: Q4 2014 CEO Ehrnrooth: 'What is the most important points with our competitors in China is that we have a good competitiveness across segments and also very broad geographic footprint. So we have pretty good market positions in most parts of the country. The objective for us has been to build a business that we are not dependent on certain geographies or certain segments to grow.' / Order book close to EUR 7B with 24.4% growth.
claims.speaker: CEO (Henrik Ehrnrooth)

claims.claim_text: コネ社の5つの開発プログラム（People Flow solutions競争力・顧客ロイヤルティ・メンテナンスパートナー・近代化事業・人材）は、競争優位性の体系的・継続的強化を制度化しており、競合が追随困難な組織学習サイクルを持つ
claims.evidence_from_transcript: Q4 2013 CEO Ehrnrooth: 'We have, for the next 3-year period, we have, again, chosen 5 development programs. The first one is we want to be the first in customer loyalty. The second one is we want to have a winning team of crew professionals. Next 3 programs are really designed to, again, further differentiate us from our competition with our product, services and solutions.' / Company has run development programs since 2008.
claims.speaker: CEO (Henrik Ehrnrooth)

claims.claim_text: コネ社はエレベーター設置の認証要件（OEM認定）が義務化された中国市場で、自社施工比率の向上を通じて保守サービスへの転換率を高める構造的機会を捉えており、法規制が競争優位性を強化する特殊ポジションにある
claims.evidence_from_transcript: Q4 2014 CEO Ehrnrooth: 'New legislation related to installation of new equipment means that anyone who installs needs to be certified and authorized by the OEM. This regulation on new installations...over time, we can increase the share of own installations that we do in the market. And that, of course, usually gives you a better possibility to convert elevators into your service base. Also we see, in many parts of China, that focus on service quality from local governments is increasing all the time, and I think that will be a positive for the OEMs.'
claims.speaker: CEO (Henrik Ehrnrooth)

claims.claim_text: コネ社は中国において現地生産・現地調達体制を完結させており、為替変動の影響を受けない競争力を持つ（ユーロ安でも中国事業の収益性に影響しない）
claims.evidence_from_transcript: Q4 2014 CEO Ehrnrooth: 'A weakening euro does not really have an impact on the margin in our China business. It does not have an impact because what we sell in China, we source and assemble and manufacture in China. So it's local cost and local revenues, very little components would come from Europe. So that doesn't really have any impact on our fundamental competitiveness in China.'
claims.speaker: CEO (Henrik Ehrnrooth)

claims.claim_text: コネ社の2014年実績（受注7000億ユーロ（近く）・EBIT率改善・Q4フリーキャッシュフロー3.68億ユーロ）は、成長と収益性を同時実現するビジネスモデルの質の高さを示している
claims.evidence_from_transcript: Q4 2014 CEO Ehrnrooth: 'Order book continued to grow, it's very strong, close to EUR 7 billion with a growth of 24.4%.' / 'EBIT for the quarter reached EUR 315 million, and our EBIT margin improved from 14.4% to 14.6%. Exceptionally strong cash flow. EUR 368 million.' / 'Growth continued to be profitable, which is of course very important and something we focus a lot on.'
claims.speaker: CEO (Henrik Ehrnrooth)

claims.claim_text: コネ社の南欧での競合バランス改善（value-based retentionアプローチ）は、価格競争に参加せず顧客セールス能力の強化で保守顧客を維持・獲得する独自の実行力を示している
claims.evidence_from_transcript: Q4 2014 CEO Ehrnrooth: 'The reason for the negative competition balance is the strong price competition we've seen, particularly in Southern Europe. I think it's a sign that we have not been willing to participate fully in this very strong price competition. Now rather than do that, we have developed our competencies, how we better retaining and finding the right units. Where we started our development in terms of our sales setups, we have competencies, we're very much in South Europe, and that's also where we've seen the best improvement. So it was only slightly negative anymore last year, very strong improvement from 2013.'
claims.speaker: CEO (Henrik Ehrnrooth)
